question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic that carries staking reward payout messages by crafting a TopicMessageFilter with a known topicId, causing the retrieve() method to return staking reward transfer details (accountId, amount, consensusTimestamp) that reveal which accounts have uncollected pending rewards, enabling targeted front-running or social-engineering attacks to redirect those rewards?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user pass throttled=false to retrieve() to bypass rate-limiting controls, enabling bulk enumeration of all historical topic messages that contain staking reward transfer records, thereby mapping every account with unpaid staking rewards without triggering throttle-based detection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user set startTime=0 in TopicMessageFilter and subscribe to a staking-reward-related topic, causing retrieve() to return the full historical record of all staking reward payouts from genesis, allowing the attacker to reconstruct the complete pending-reward state of every account and identify high-value targets for theft?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user set limit=0 (hasLimit() returns false) in TopicMessageFilter to remove the upper bound on returned messages, causing retrieve() to stream an unbounded number of staking reward topic messages and exhausting server resources while simultaneously harvesting all unpaid reward data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with endTime set just before the current consensus timestamp to retrieve all staking reward messages up to that point, then use the returned data to identify accounts whose pending rewards have not yet been claimed, enabling targeted theft attempts before the next reward distribution?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user open multiple concurrent gRPC subscriptions each with a different subscriberId (auto-generated via RandomStringUtils) and overlapping TopicMessageFilter time ranges, causing the mirror node to serve staking reward topic messages in parallel streams, effectively multiplying the data extraction rate and bypassing per-connection throttling?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that subscriberId is randomly generated client-side (RandomStringUtils.random) and never validated server-side, allowing the attacker to supply a crafted subscriberId that collides with an existing legitimate subscriber's session, potentially hijacking or duplicating that subscriber's staking reward message stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user set startTime to a value equal to DomainUtils.now() minus one nanosecond, bypassing the isValidStartTime() check (startTime <= DomainUtils.now()), and then use retrieve() to stream real-time staking reward topic messages as they are written, enabling the attacker to observe unpaid reward distributions the moment they occur?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication on the gRPC retrieve() endpoint to enumerate all topicIds that carry staking reward data by iterating EntityId values in TopicMessageFilter, then subscribing to each discovered topic to harvest unpaid reward information without any credential requirement?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user use the METRIC constant 'hiero_mirror_grpc_retriever' to query exposed Prometheus/Micrometer metrics endpoints and infer the volume and timing of staking reward topic message retrievals, thereby deducing when large reward payouts are being processed and timing an attack to intercept unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with a very large limit value combined with throttled=false to force the PollingTopicMessageRetriever to execute maxPolls iterations against the database, each returning staking reward topic messages, causing a denial-of-service that delays legitimate reward processing while the attacker harvests reward data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cold-publisher nature of retrieve() (which only executes on subscription) to open and cancel subscriptions in rapid succession, causing repeated database queries for staking reward topic messages that reveal the exact consensus timestamps of pending reward transfers, enabling precise timing attacks?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a future timestamp in TopicMessageFilter (isValidEndTime() only checks endTime > startTime, not that endTime is in the past), causing retrieve() to keep the subscription open indefinitely and stream all future staking reward topic messages as they arrive, giving the attacker a persistent real-time feed of unpaid reward data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux<TopicMessage> reactive stream returned by retrieve() to apply client-side backpressure manipulation, slowing consumption to keep the server-side subscription alive for an extended period while continuously receiving staking reward topic messages, effectively maintaining a long-lived unauthorized data tap on reward activity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TopicMessageFilter with topicId pointing to the Hedera staking reward account's associated topic (e.g., topic linked to account 0.0.800), causing retrieve() to return all messages related to the staking reward fund, revealing the total reserved rewards and enabling calculation of which accounts have the largest uncollected balances?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of per-user rate limiting on the retrieve() interface to open thousands of concurrent gRPC streams with different TopicMessageFilter time windows, reconstructing the complete staking reward history across all accounts and identifying every account with pending unpaid rewards for targeted exploitation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user use the retrieve() method with throttled=true and a very small startTime to force the PollingTopicMessageRetriever to poll indefinitely (numRepeats=Long.MAX_VALUE), consuming server thread pool resources from the bounded elastic scheduler and causing a resource exhaustion that delays legitimate staking reward processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isValidEndTime() validation (endTime == null || endTime > startTime) by passing endTime=null to retrieve() to remove the time upper bound, causing the retriever to stream all staking reward topic messages from startTime to the present and beyond, with no temporal restriction on the data returned?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMessageFilter.subscriberId field (generated by RandomStringUtils with SecureRandom) to perform a birthday-attack collision against existing subscriber IDs, causing the server to associate the attacker's retrieve() call with a legitimate subscriber's session and potentially receiving that subscriber's staking reward message stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user use the gRPC retrieve() interface to perform a timing side-channel attack by measuring the response latency for different topicId values in TopicMessageFilter, inferring which topics have recent staking reward activity and thus which accounts are about to receive unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of message content filtering in retrieve() to receive raw TopicMessage payloads that contain encoded staking reward transfer data (accountId, amount, consensusTimestamp), and decode these to identify accounts with large unpaid staking rewards before those rewards are distributed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with startTime set to the exact epoch day boundary (start of a staking period) to retrieve all topic messages published during a reward calculation window, enabling reconstruction of the pending reward amounts for all staking accounts before the rewards are paid out?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux reactive stream from retrieve() combined with the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...)) behavior in PollingTopicMessageRetriever to keep a failed subscription retrying indefinitely, maintaining persistent unauthorized access to staking reward topic messages even after transient errors?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that retrieve() accepts any valid EntityId as topicId without verifying the caller has authorization to read that topic, allowing enumeration of private or restricted topics that contain staking reward configuration data, revealing reward rate parameters used to calculate unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user use retrieve() with throttled=false and maxPolls set to a large value to perform a high-frequency polling attack against the topic message database, generating excessive load that delays the importer's ability to write new staking reward transfer records, causing reward processing delays that could be exploited?
